dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: mcp-server/src/pipecat_mcp_server/channel.py lines 85-89 mutates sys.path at import time to expose top-level shared/ and server/ packages to a workspace member that does not declare them as dependenci
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Top level holds two parallel server packages: server/ (local-audio voice daemon, LLM backends, voice_switcher feature, config defaults) and mcp-server/src/pipecat_mcp_server/ (talky daemon, in-process
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: server/backends/ sibling drift: moltis.py and openclaw.py define services that inherit Pipecat's LLMService and live as full backends; pi.py defines PiLLMService that does NOT inherit LLMService and i
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: server/logging_config.py:70 has a bare `except:` that silently swallows setLevel failures inside a per-logger loop — the only bare-except in production code, the rest of the codebase uses typed/Except
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Function name vocabulary is dominated by intent-revealing verbs: ensure_daemon, restore_profile_on_startup, cleanup_legacy_daemon, _emit_profile_changed, _schedule_ttl_if_empty, switch_to_profile, req
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: server/backends/base.py defines BaseLLMBackend (ABC), SimpleBackendMixin, DualPipelineMixin, BackendManager (147 LOC) but the only references are inside that file itself; MoltisLLMService and OpenClaw
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: Root pyproject.toml pins pipecat-ai==0.0.102 with extras [runner,silero,webrtc] but mcp-server/pyproject.toml uses pipecat-ai>=0.0.101 with a much larger extras set ([cartesia,deepgram,google,local-sm
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: channel.py: VoiceChannel internals are well-sectioned (── attach / detach ──, ── speak / listen ──) and use precise asyncio.wait races for listen() and request_leave() with always-cancelled pending ta
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: shared/daemon_protocol.py defines length-prefixed JSON helpers (send_message/recv_message) and unix-socket path constants (VOICE_SOCKET_PATH/VOICE_PID_FILE) that server/tts_client.py and server/voice_
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Production Python ~8.3K LOC; only ~1.1K LOC of real pytest tests across 6 files (test_config.py, test_daemon_protocol.py, test_channel_ttl.py, test_request_leave.py, test_channel_greeting.py, test_aut
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: mcp-server/src/pipecat_mcp_server/server.py: 12 MCP tools all decorated `@mcp.tool()` and uniformly `async def` — consistent shape at the boundary
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Comments throughout the codebase explain WHY rather than restating WHAT — extensive ticket-number references (e.g. 'Ticket b3c4', '58db', '0b80', '76a3') tie code to design decisions in voice_daemon.p
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: server/tts_daemon.py is a backward-compat wrapper re-exporting from voice_daemon, but no Python module imports it (grep for 'from server.tts_daemon' / 'import tts_daemon' returns zero callers). Only f
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: shared/ is a coherent utility layer with avg_fan_in 6.8 and one-way import direction from every other directory (server/, mcp-server/, root). No reciprocal/bidirectional edges in coupling_matrix — cle
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: shared/profile_manager.py:475 eagerly creates a module-level singleton via `profile_manager = get_profile_manager()` at import time. The constructor performs file I/O — mkdir(~/.talky), copies bundled
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: server.py: thin MCP tool layer (each @mcp.tool one-line delegation to voice_channel) plus header-commented sectioning — patterns are consistent.
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: mcp-server/src/pipecat_mcp_server/channel.py: VoiceChannel methods (speak, listen, request_leave, is_live, status, switch_to_profile) declare return types and document raise conditions, edge cases, an
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: AST scan across server/, shared/, mcp-server/src, talky_cli.py, talky_auth.py found no identical if/else branches, no `if x: return True else: return False` patterns, no dead code after return/raise,
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: shared/profile_manager.py:447 — resolve_talky_profile passes tp.llm_backend (Optional[str]) into get_llm_backend(name: str); same at line 450 for voice_profile. The accessor signature lies — None is r

review_issue_summaries.summary: mcp-server package reaches into top-level shared/ and server/ via sys.path.insert at import time
mcp-server/services_factory.py duplicates shared/service_factory.py with a sys.path shim and a dead import path
channel.py defers profile_manager imports 9 times inside methods, signaling hub/cycle pressure
voice_daemon (local audio) and talky daemon (mcp-server) are parallel subsystems with no shared lifecycle/boundary
Package named 'server/' is consumed by mcp-server/ (the real server) and holds an unrelated mix of voice daemon, backends, and features
Two services_factory implementations (shared/service_factory.py and mcp-server/.../services_factory.py) own overlapping STT/TTS construction
Legacy TTS-daemon and /api/profile compat layers persist with no removal gate, blurring 'what is the current architecture'
talky_cli.py at repo root is 931 LOC with mixed dispatch responsibilities — root sprawl rather than a cli/ package
mcp-server/.../server.py (846 LOC) blends route definitions, MCP tool registration, lifecycle, and profile API in one file
CLAUDE.md describes architecture by ticket-hash narrative, making decomposition rationale undecodable without git archaeology
server/features/ is a 'plural' package containing a single file (voice_switcher.py) — false decomposition
server/backends/ siblings follow three different structural protocols
server/backends/base.py defines a parallel abstraction (BaseLLMBackend, mixins, BackendManager, singleton) that no production code uses
shared/service_factory.py and mcp-server/services_factory.py — singular vs plural, with sys.path hack
from __future__ import annotations applied in 5/7 mcp-server modules but missing in two siblings
services_factory raises ValueError without `from e`, dropping the original traceback at the user-facing setup boundary
OpenClaw backend raises inside except without `from err` and has multiple log-only swallow sites that hide backend failures
Bare `except:` in logging_config silently swallows setLevel failures
MCP tools inconsistently return `{"error": ...}` dicts vs propagating exceptions
available_profiles silently returns just the MCP driver if ProfileManager raises
daemon_is_running is defined in two daemon contexts with the same bare name
BaseLLMBackend / BackendManager / mixins in server/backends/base.py have zero concrete users
create_pi_service is a pass-through factory with no callers
create_vad_analyzer is a one-liner wrapping SileroVADAnalyzer()
server/tts_daemon.py is a 26-line backward-compat re-export with no in-tree callers
'rich>=13.0.0' declared in root pyproject.toml but never imported
requests and httpx declared as mcp-server runtime deps but never imported by talky code
kokoro-onnx is a hard runtime dep of mcp-server though kokoro TTS is also gated behind the lazy-install 'tts-kokoro' extra
pipecat-ai pinned exactly (==0.0.102) in root but loose (>=0.0.101) in mcp-server workspace member
mcp-server hard-requires pipecat-ai with [cartesia, deepgram, google, websocket, ...] disabling lazy-install benefits
build_device_auth (v2) is dead code duplicating build_device_auth_v3 with magic-byte SPKI extraction repeated verbatim
_handle_messages mixes streaming/final/fallback branches with hasattr-based state and three repeated put_nowait try/excepts
Same-provider vs cross-provider switching logic is implemented three times across handle_set_voice_profile, switch_profile, and channel.switch_voice
cmd_pi and cmd_claude define identical inner `class Args` shims to repackage args
_generate_session_key has elif branches with identical bodies and a duplicated default
channel._build_and_start_pipeline is a ~210-line method assembling pipeline plus inline pong-responder closure
VoiceProfileSwitcher.cleanup duplicates a try/except block for close vs shutdown methods
_process_user_text nests two try/excepts that each push LLMFullResponseEndFrame, risking a double end frame on response failure
mcp-server/daemon_bridge.py reimplements unix-socket length-prefix protocol and path constants that shared/daemon_protocol.py already exports
Voice-profile switching logic exists in both VoiceChannel.switch_voice and VoiceProfileSwitcher._handle_set_voice_profile/switch_profile
talky_cli.py repeats the same urllib request + HTTPError + URLError + json.loads block across cmd_profile, cmd_voice, cmd_talkystatus, and others
lsof-based port-holder discovery is implemented in both talky_cli._kill_pids_on_port and server._check_ports_or_exit
server/tests/test_config.py imports non-existent server.config.profile_manager — entire file fails at collection
Three test_*.py files are print-driven scripts with no assertions and won't be discovered by pytest
talky_cli.py — 931 LOC, complexity 112, sole user-facing entry — has zero tests
mcp-server/server.py (846 LOC, complexity 230) and channel pipeline build are untested
shared/profile_manager.py — 19 importers, 475 LOC — has no direct tests
server/voice_daemon.py, backends/openclaw.py, backends/moltis.py, say_command.py, transcribe.py have no real tests
MCP tools use three incompatible success/error envelopes across siblings
convo_speak returns bool while every other convo_* tool returns dict

dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: Single-user local desktop app — no multi-tenant DB, no users/roles, no RLS surface. Credentials live in ~/.talky/credentials/*.json (talky_auth.py) and are read uniformly by backends.

payload.strategy_summary: Cluster-based execution prioritizing high-impact subjective dimensions. Order: (1) test-strategy-bootstrap front-loads the one-line server/tests/test_config.py import fix to unblock pytest collection and the test_strategy dimension. (2) legacy-shim-deletion-sweep deletes server/tts_daemon.py, server/backends/base.py, profile_manager singleton, create_pi_service, and legacy_daemon_is_running — pure deletions, zero callers verified. (3) small-fixes-batch absorbs trivial typed-tedium across 13 issues in one PR, plus the auto/unused autofix sweep. (4) shared-package-and-syspath promotes shared/ to a workspace member and drops sys.path inserts, prerequisite for (5) services-factory-unification and (6) channel-and-server-decomposition (server.py 846 LOC split into routes/tools/lifespan/server). (7) cli-split-and-helpers splits talky_cli.py 931 LOC into a cli/ package with _daemon_get/_daemon_post helpers. (8) package-restructure flattens server/features/, consolidates scripts/, reorgs server/ into purpose folders. (9) websocket-backend-base extracts a base class for Moltis/OpenClaw, cleans up OpenClaw handlers and Moltis branches. (10) voice-switcher-consolidation extracts apply_profile and deletes RTVI-era handlers. (11) mcp-tool-contract-unification adopts {success, error, payload} envelope across MCP tools. (12) dependency-trim drops rich/requests/httpx, aligns pipecat-ai pin to ==0.0.102, demotes kokoro-onnx. Defer worktree-isolated security findings (auto/security-B603/B607/B404/B101/B606/stale_exclude). Verification: each cluster lands as one PR with ruff+pyright+pytest green, manual smoke test of voice profile switching and MCP tool dispatch after structural clusters land.

cluster_summaries.name: test-strategy-bootstrap
cluster_summaries.thesis: Fix broken test_config import path (single-edit unblock), expand profile_manager test coverage, add CLI/daemon smoke tests, retire fake-print test files

cluster_summaries.name: legacy-shim-deletion-sweep
cluster_summaries.thesis: Delete dead modules with zero in-tree callers: server/tts_daemon.py, server/backends/base.py, eager profile_manager singleton, legacy_daemon_is_running predicate, create_pi_service factory, AsyncGener

cluster_summaries.name: shared-package-and-syspath
cluster_summaries.thesis: Promote shared/ to a real workspace member, drop sys.path inserts, hoist deferred profile_manager imports, collapse duplicate daemon_is_running names, fold profile_manager triple-branch construction,

cluster_summaries.name: services-factory-unification
cluster_summaries.thesis: Collapse to one factory in shared/, delete mcp-server services_factory wrapper, add from-e chaining and return annotations, fix singular/plural naming drift

cluster_summaries.name: channel-and-server-decomposition
cluster_summaries.thesis: Split mcp-server/server.py (846 LOC) into routes/tools/lifespan/server, extract pipeline build helpers in channel.py and voice_daemon._do_listen, extract _read_room_setting helper, move _http_sessions

cluster_summaries.name: cli-split-and-helpers
cluster_summaries.thesis: Create cli/ package, split talky_cli.py (931 LOC) by subcommand group, factor _daemon_get/_daemon_post/_resolve_daemon_env helpers, collapse cmd_pi/cmd_claude duplication, drop inner-class Args copypa

cluster_summaries.name: package-restructure
cluster_summaries.thesis: Flatten server/features/, consolidate /server/scripts/ into root /scripts/, address inverted server/ package role and protocol drift in server/backends/ siblings via naming/docs (not new abstraction)

cluster_summaries.name: websocket-backend-base
cluster_summaries.thesis: Extract WebsocketLLMBackendBase from Moltis/OpenClaw, normalize handle_messages, remove magic-byte v2 dup, single finally for _process_user_text, narrow moltis debug log, collapse session-key dead bra

cluster_summaries.name: voice-switcher-consolidation
cluster_summaries.thesis: Extract single VoiceProfileSwitcher.apply_profile, slim switcher to still-used surface (delete RTVI-era handlers), iterate (close,shutdown) with shared try/except

cluster_summaries.name: mcp-tool-contract-unification
cluster_summaries.thesis: Pick one envelope ({success, error?, ...payload}), apply to convo_speak/switch_profile/switch_voice, document at top of tools.py

cluster_summaries.name: dependency-trim
cluster_summaries.thesis: Drop unused rich, unused requests/httpx in mcp-server, align pipecat-ai pin to ==0.0.102 across workspace, narrow pipecat-ai extras, demote kokoro-onnx to optional

cluster_summaries.name: small-fixes-batch
cluster_summaries.thesis: Trivial typed-tedium and small cleanups bundled into one PR: Optional[str] annotations, Pi NoReturn, dict[str, Any] daemon protocol, narrow bare except, drop unused imports, inline trivial wrappers, d

payload.strategy_summary: Active queue: legacy-shim-deletion-sweep (primary, 9 issues, 5 file-scoped delete steps targeting Cross-module arch headroom) and auto/unused autofix (42 items) as parallel cleanup. All other 9 review clusters (services-factory-unification, channel-and-server-decomposition, cli-split-and-helpers, package-restructure, shared-package-and-syspath, websocket-backend-base, voice-switcher-consolidation, mcp-tool-contract-unification, dependency-trim, small-fixes-batch) deferred to bake under strategist re-churn guard. Bandit security and test-coverage auto-clusters skipped per documented per-cluster reasons.

payload.strategy_summary: Promote all 11 prior-cycle manual clusters (77 review issues) into the active queue led by small-fixes-batch for immediate Code-quality recovery, then architectural sweeps (shared-package-and-syspath, services-factory-unification, channel-and-server-decomposition, cli-split-and-helpers) for Cross-module-arch / High-elegance / Init-coupling, followed by websocket-backend-base, voice-switcher-consolidation, mcp-tool-contract-unification, package-restructure, dependency-trim, legacy-shim-deletion-sweep; permanently skip 14 auto-clusters (security bandit, unused, coverage, stale_exclude) as low-value or already-handled by ruff and test-strategy-bootstrap.
